creator: feld@FreeBSD.org
text:
Created attachment 177543
packet capture

We have encountered an issue reproducible on FreeBSD 10.3 with read-only NFS mounts. FreeBSD is sending NLM_UNLOCKs when mounted read-only. This was discovered because it triggers a bug in NetApp filers where it causes the file to be truncated.

We can trigger this reliably when using clamscan to scan virus samples on a readonly NFS mount.

Other FreeBSD versions have not been tested, but we did not believe this happened in FreeBSD 9.3. I believe there was a lot of NFS related development between 9.0 and 10.0, but I am not sure if this bug was introduced because of those changes.

Attached is a pcap showing UNLOCKs sent.
